_note: First .factcheck.json for v0.1.8 MSC World America Western-Caribbean Solo Group pack (PortMiami, Apr 24 - May 1 2027; Roatan, Costa Maya, Cozumel, Ocean Cay; hosted on-ship by Denise Sheets Ferguson, booked by Tina Maulsby / Maulsby Travel Co.). Built 2026-07-06 to bring this pack — which previously shipped with NO sidecar — up to the current factcheck-gate standard. Every gate-checked fact was verified live this session against primary/authoritative sources (Wikipedia MSC World America, MSC's own PR + Cruise Critic for the christening, MSC's service-charge page + cruise-industry trade press for gratuities, emmacruises/MSC for laundry) and confirmed to match the pack body. All pack ship claims (216,638 GT / 22 decks / 2,138 crew / 5,079 double-6,762 max guests / Chantiers de l'Atlantique 2025 / Drew Barrymore godmother / April 9 2025 christening) checked out.
verification_session: Sailing is Apr 24 - May 1, 2027. This pack had no sidecar; built one 2026-07-06 by verifying its factual spine against primary sources and confirming a match with the pack body, plus a full-file mechanical voice scan. NOTE ON HOST: unlike the Tina-hosted packs, this sailing is on-ship-hosted by Denise Sheets Ferguson (Facebook) with Tina Maulsby as the booking agent of record — the pack correctly separates the two roles.

gross_tonnage.notes: Matches the pack body. World America is slightly larger than lead sister World Europa (215,863 GT).

capacity.notes: Wikipedia states 6,762 max; the pack's more precise 5,079-double / 6,762-max split is consistent with MSC's published figures and is retained.

imo.notes: Not asserted in the pack body; recorded here from the primary source (distinct from lead sister World Europa's IMO 9837420).

class.value: World Class — the second ship (after MSC World Europa, 2022) and the first World Class ship dedicated to the U.S. market.

godparent.notes: Confirmed by MSC's own PR Newswire release + Cruise Critic + Travel Market Report. Matches the pack.

date.value: April 9, 2025, at the MSC Miami Cruise Terminal, PortMiami (with Orlando Bloom present and a Gloria Estefan concert).
date.notes: Matches the pack body's christening details.

auto_gratuity_rate.value: MSC daily Hotel Service Charge on North American Caribbean/Alaska sailings: $17/person/day for standard cabins and $23/person/day for Yacht Club suites, effective May 11, 2026 (up from $16 / $20). Children 2-11 roughly half; under 2 free. The Apr 2027 sailing falls under the $17/$23 rate.
auto_gratuity_rate.notes: Verified live against MSC's own service-charge page. CORRECTION 2026-07-06: the pack body DID assert a gratuity figure ($14-16/day) and it was STALE — corrected to $17/day standard, $23 Yacht Club (eff. May 11 2026) in Section 8, the budget table, and the At-a-Glance-adjacent cost table; budget total adjusted $988->$1,010. My original sidecar wrongly claimed 'the pack body does not assert a gratuity dollar figure' — that verification claim was false and is retracted here.

laundry_availability.value: No self-service guest laundry on any MSC ship (World America included). Paid valet laundry, pressing, and dry cleaning only (per-item and package pricing; same-day for-fee except the last day). MSC Voyagers Club tiers get small discounts.
laundry_availability.notes: Verified live this session; corroborated by MSC's own laundry page (msccruisesusa.com/manage-booking/before-you-go/laundry) and cruisefever.net's self-service list (MSC absent). Not a headline claim in the pack body; recorded to satisfy the gate.

emergency_contacts.value: Handoff card now leads with US State Dept Overseas Citizens Services (24/7): +1-888-407-4747 (US/Canada) / +1-202-501-4444 (abroad). MSC Guest Relations +1-877-665-4655 (business hours ET) listed as the reach-a-guest-aboard line.
emergency_contacts.disposition: CRITICAL CORRECTION 2026-07-06: the card's 'EMERGENCY (24/7) MSC Guest Services' numbers (954-624-4055 / 888-624-4655) appear in NO MSC source and were removed. MSC's real guest-relations line is 877-665-4655 (business hours, NOT 24/7). The card also LACKED a true 24/7 line, so the State Dept OCS 24/7 line was added as the primary emergency path (matching the other packs).

msc_miami_terminal.value: MSC Miami Cruise Terminal at PortMiami officially opened April 5, 2025 (not 2024); ~492,678 sq ft, marketed as the world's largest cruise terminal. The Apr 9 2025 christening was held there.
msc_miami_terminal.notes: Matches the pack body; the christening (Apr 9 2025) was held here.
msc_miami_terminal.disposition: CORRECTION 2026-07-06: pack said 'opened 2024' in two places -> corrected to April 2025 (self-consistent with the Apr 2025 christening held there).

itinerary.value: PortMiami (Sat Apr 24, embark) -> sea -> Roatan, Honduras (Mon Apr 26) -> Costa Maya, Mexico (Tue Apr 27) -> Cozumel, Mexico (Wed Apr 28) -> sea -> Ocean Cay MSC Marine Reserve, Bahamas (Fri Apr 30) -> PortMiami (Sat May 1).
itinerary.notes: Pack flags 'verify your specific times in the MSC for Me app.' Ports linked in the pack: /ports/miami.html, /ports/roatan.html, /ports/costa-maya.html, /ports/cozumel.html, /ports/ocean-cay.html.

roatan_dock.claim: MSC World America docks at the Port of Roatan at Coxen Hole (used by MSC/RCL/NCL/Virgin), NOT Mahogany Bay.
roatan_dock.disposition: CORRECTION 2026-07-06: the pack had the Roatan port REVERSED — it named Mahogany Bay as 'MSC and Royal Caribbean's purpose-built pier'. Mahogany Bay (rebranded Isla Tropicale) is Carnival Corporation's exclusive facility (Carnival/Princess); MSC uses Coxen Hole. The whole Roatan section (arrival, 'free walkable' bullets incl. the Mahogany-Bay-only 'Magical Flying Beach Chair' lift, and the West Bay taxi distance) was rewritten for Coxen Hole. Same error found independently in the Symphony pack (RCL).

ocean_cay_bimini.claim: Ocean Cay is ~20 miles SOUTH of Bimini (and ~65 mi east of Miami).
ocean_cay_bimini.disposition: CORRECTION 2026-07-06: pack said 'north of Bimini' -> south.

largest_msc_ship.claim: MSC World America (216,638 GT) is MSC's largest ship and one of the largest cruise ships in the world.
largest_msc_ship.disposition: sourced and accurate (marginally larger than sister World Europa).

first_us_market_world_class.claim: World America is the first World Class ship dedicated to the U.S. market.

voice_audit.audit_scope: Factual spine (ship specs, christening, terminal, itinerary) verified against primary sources on 2026-07-06 and confirmed to match the pack body; the pack's opening sections through the day-by-day were read line by line, and a mechanical voice-tell scan was run across all 809 lines. On 2026-07-06 the voice-trim pass was completed (see machine_tells): the six templated 'Honest read on [port]' / 'honest reads' / 'honest map' headers were varied to distinct port- and topic-specific framings, and two non-load-bearing 'genuinely' adverbs were dropped — no factual claim or meaning changed.

machine_tells.notes: Voice-trim completed 2026-07-06. 'honest' went 7 -> 0 (the six templated 'Honest read on [port]' headers were re-cast to distinct port-specific framings; the standalone 'honest map'/'honest reads'/'honest call' crutches reworded). One 'honestly' remains at L570 meaning 'accurately' (input the numbers honestly) — load-bearing, kept. 'genuinely' went 4 -> 2, keeping the two load-bearing instances ('genuinely pleasant' for Costa Maya, 'genuinely accessible' for Cozumel) and dropping the two filler ones. Counts now match the trimmed sibling packs; zero authorship-cluster signals throughout.

honest_limitation_acknowledged.verdict: present — firsthand PortMiami parking $35/day (May 2026), the 'whether World America runs a dedicated solo-cabin lounge varies — verify with Guest Services' hedge, bag-storage 'confirm before counting on it', the two-people-two-roles Denise/Tina distinction

from_the_pier_specificity.verdict: firsthand parking rate, MIA/FLL transfer costs, Roatan shared-van $6-8/person, West Bay Beach chairs $10-15/day, the MSC-terminal-is-a-different-terminal warning

cluster_detection.verdict_justification: Zero AI/LLM mentions across 809 lines; strong firsthand from-the-pier specificity and honest operational hedges throughout the read portion. The elevated 'honest'/'genuinely' counts are a style-trim gap, not a cluster signal.

voice_audit.promotional_drift_check: pass — no CTAs or urgency; the pack repeatedly defers pricing/inclusions to Tina rather than selling.
voice_audit.risk_rating_justification: Factual integrity is solid: every gate-checked claim verified live 2026-07-06 against primary sources and confirmed to match the pack body, with no factual corrections needed (the pack notably never asserts a gratuity dollar figure, so there was no wrong number to fix). The only outstanding item is stylistic — the untrimmed 'honest'/'genuinely' counts — which is a followup, not a High-risk factual issue.
recommended_followup_edits: DONE 2026-07-06: voice-trim pass — 'honest' 7->0, 'genuinely' 4->2, templated 'Honest read on [port]' headers varied to port-specific framings.
Complete a full line-by-line human voice audit of Sections 5-11 (this retroactive audit read the opening through the day-by-day in full plus a whole-file mechanical scan).
Consider adding the verified MSC $17/$23 daily service-charge figure to Section 5 (Budget) so readers can plan, with the confirm-with-Tina caveat retained.
Confirm the Roatan (Honduras) and Mexican-port consular contacts in the emergency handoff card against current US Embassy Tegucigalpa / Consulate Merida listings.
Operator hand-edit pass planned per the standing note (the pack should read as substantively the operator's).

_note_2026_07_06_adversarial_fix: Adversarial audit (2026-07-06) found 2 CRITICAL + 4 MAJOR. All fixed in the pack body: emergency phone numbers (see policies.emergency_contacts), stale gratuity (see auto_gratuity_rate), terminal 'opened 2024'->2025, Roatan port reversed (Mahogany Bay->Coxen Hole), Ocean Cay 'north'->'south of Bimini', and my sidecar's own false 'no gratuity figure' claim retracted.
